name: Can I get workers comp if my lung problems started from dust at my factory job?
description: Yes, if doctor links it to work exposure. Workers' comp covers medical and wages for occupational lung issues[2][4].

mainEntity.name: Can I get workers comp if my lung problems started from dust at my factory job?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if doctor links it to work exposure. Workers' comp covers medical and wages for occupational lung issues[2][4].
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can qualify for workers' compensation if medical evidence shows your respiratory condition arose from workplace exposure by a preponderance of evidence. Workers' comp provides medical benefits and wage replacement for occupational illnesses like asthma or COPD from dust or chemicals[2][4]. It applies even for gradual exposures, but you typically can't sue your employer directly. - Prove 'arising out of employment' with doctor opinions, exposure history, and OSHA records. - Pre-existing conditions may complicate but don't bar claims if work aggravated them. - Strict filing deadlines apply, often 1-2 years from diagnosis. Report symptoms immediately, get a qualified medical evaluation, and file your claim to secure benefits without delay.
